name: Zonules.com Claim Registry
note: Every scientific claim is supported by a registered source, marked as conceptual interpretation, marked as internal framework, or excluded. No unsourced scientific claim may be published.

claims.statement: The zonules of Zinn are fine fibrous strands that suspend the crystalline lens from the ciliary body.
The zonular apparatus, also called the suspensory ligament of the lens, holds the lens in centered geometric tension.
Tension transmitted through the zonules allows the ciliary muscle to change lens shape during accommodation, shifting focus across distances.
The structure is named after Johann Gottfried Zinn, the 18th-century anatomist who described it.
The zonules are the literal instance of the suspension layer (FIO-01): focus is produced by hidden structural tension, not by the lens alone.
